last_run.notes: Profiled WAL (I/O-bound), analyzed txn codegen. Added txn-escape-analysis to backlog.

key_findings: WAL encoder.encode 32% cum - blocked by DEFER (cannot inline), I/O-bound (40% syscall)
WAL write() dominated by syscall and GC, not compute-bound
txn Range: 627 allocs/op for 100 keys, escape analysis may help
txn checkLease cost 83, Count cost 92, IsTxnReadonly cost 92 - near threshold
mvcc: cmpbody 35% flat (asm), runtime.mapassign 43% - not compiler-optimizable
All remaining mvcc BCE blocked by function calls or struct copies clobbering memory
backend tx_buffer.go: 20+ BCE blocked by struct field aliasing
Optimization frontier largely exhausted for mvcc and WAL at SSA level
Remaining opportunities require interprocedural analysis or major infrastructure
